summary: Justin Brennan covers foundational business systems for launching a multifamily operating company, including entity formation, accounting software, CRM/investor portals, websites, and lead generation funnels. While not directly addressing Brian's deal sourcing or underwriting drag, these systems are prerequisites for scaling a multifamily business.
takeaways: Form your LLC in the state where you live, not where properties are located, to avoid tax issues and establish your holdings company
You can form an LLC directly via Secretary of State website for $100-200 instead of paying LegalZoom $1000+
Use QuickBooks for accounting software and a CRM+portal system like SyndicationPro ($100s vs thousands) to manage investors and leads
Build website on Wix.com or hire freelancers on Fiverr.com for $100-300 to create professional frontage
Create lead funnels and lead magnets (content, meetup groups, educational resources) to attract investors and partners into your ecosystem
When bringing in other investors, create a single-purpose LLC for each asset in the state where the property is located
You need both analytical/operational and visionary personality types on your team—hire or partner with the type you lack

skill_candidates.change: Establish professional business infrastructure (LLC, CRM, website, accounting) before focusing on deal sourcing
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin emphasizes that investors trust operators with organized systems in place; having these fundamentals allows you to 'look professional to investors' and scale operations when ready

title: Lesson 1: Business Systems
module: Module 1B: Creating A Business LLC & Team
